title: ANALYTICAL CHEMISTRY OF ZIRCONIUM. PART I. BENZILIC ACID
author: M. VENKATARAMANIAH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Pure zirconyl chloride was obtained from zircon by a combination of the procedures given by Rossitter and Sanders and Hillebrand and Lundell. It is shown that benzilic acid precipitates zirconium from solutions up to 0.225  N  in HCl. Separation from most divalent and trivalent elements occurs in a single precipitation with the reagent in 0.20  N  HCI. In other cases (except tin and titanium) a second precipitation yields a pure zirconium compound. The precipitate of zirconium benzilate has the approximate composition,  \(O=Zr<{^{OH}_B}\)  4H 2 O and is a basic salt.

The precipitate of zirconium benzilate has the approximate composition,  \(O=Zr<{^{OH}_B}\)  4H 2 O and is a basic salt.
